title: Fixing Missing Left Child Node Handling in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree, write a function to calculate the height of each node from the root node downwards. The height of a node is the number of edges on the longest path from the root to the node.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a root node of a binary tree, each node is an object with a value (n) and two children, left and right.
statement.outputFormat: Function should return an array of heights from the root node to each node downwards.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity should be O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the tree.

examples.input: {value: 1, left: null, right: {value: 2, left: null, right: null}}
examples.explanation: Height of root node is 1 (only it), height of leaf node is 1 (only it).

hints.text: Start from the root node and traverse the tree using DFS, updating the height of each node.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Missing Left Child Node Handling in a Binary Tree
editorial.intuition: To calculate the height of each node, we can start from the root node and traverse the tree using DFS. We update the height of each node by getting the maximum height of its left and right subtrees and adding 1.
editorial.bruteForce: This problem can be solved using a Brute Force approach by traversing the tree and calculating the height of each node from scratch.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use DFS to traverse the tree and update the height of each node in a single pass.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution is correct because it calculates the height of each node by getting the maximum height of its left and right subtrees and adding 1.
editorial.dryRun: Start from the root node and traverse the tree using DFS, updating the height of each node.
commonMistakes: Not updating the height of each node
Not using DFS to traverse the tree

solutions.python:
class Solution:
	pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
	import sys
	import json
	input = sys.stdin.readline
	solve = Solution()
	root = json.loads(input().strip())
	print(solve.binaryTreeHeight(root))
